summary: Completed testing of BLOCK 167 (Multi-copy Discogs import feature). All tests passed. Backend correctly accepts instance_id, computes copy_number/total_copies for records with same discogs_id, and frontend displays 'Copy X of Y' badges correctly.
critical_code_review_comments: Code review passed: instance_id field properly added to RecordCreate/RecordResponse models
Code review passed: Import logic at lines 1143-1155 correctly checks instance_id for duplicate detection
Code review passed: copy_number/total_copies computed correctly in GET /api/records (lines 138-147)
Code review passed: Feed filters source != discogs_import at line 395 in hive.py
seed_data_creation: Temporarily created 2 test records with same discogs_id (14215683) but different instance_ids (111111, 222222) to verify multi-copy badges. Records deleted after test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: BLOCK 167 multi-copy import feature fully tested. Backend: POST /api/records accepts instance_id, GET /api/records returns copy_number/total_copies for records sharing same discogs_id. Frontend: CollectionPage.js shows 'Copy X of Y' badge (data-testid='copy-badge-{id}') on cards with total_copies > 1. Records with unique discogs_id correctly show null copy fields. Feed integrity maintained via source filter.

backend_instance_id_field.details: POST /api/records accepts instance_id field and returns it in response

backend_copy_number_computation.details: GET /api/records computes copy_number (1-N) and total_copies for records with same discogs_id

backend_unique_records_null_copy.details: Records with unique discogs_id have null copy_number and null total_copies

backend_different_notes_per_copy.details: Each copy can have different notes field values

frontend_copy_badge_display.details: Collection page shows 'Copy X of Y' badge on cards with total_copies > 1 using data-testid='copy-badge-{id}'

frontend_no_badge_unique_records.details: Records with unique discogs_id do not show copy badges
